title: Boeing (BA)Intrinsic Value
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on Boeing using normalized cash flows. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what Boeing is really worth.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-hrad4euh>
Applied to our $3.5B normalized base, a 15% CAGR brings Year 5 FCF to ~$7B. Boeing generated over $13B in FCF in 2018 before the crashes. $7B represents a realistic, partial return to historical profitability as delivery volumes of the 737 MAX and 787 eventually scale and stabilize.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-hrad4euh>
10Y Treasury: 4.18%. Boeing's beta is elevated due to constant operational shocks. While a textbook WACC might be slightly lower due to the heavy debt weight, equity investors should demand at least 10% to underwrite the substantial execution and regulatory risks of a turnaround.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-hrad4euh>
A 2.5% terminal growth rate reflects a mature industrial giant growing slightly below global GDP. It acknowledges the durable global duopoly with Airbus, but bakes in long-term constraints on pricing power due to intense airline customer pushback and persistent regulatory oversight.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use a "Normalized" FCF instead of actual numbers?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "Boeing (BA) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 19, 2026.
